tweets.title: R to @sidhant: On death of Indian Seafarers Full statement: As per information made available to us from the Directorate General of Maritime Administration, India, MV Golden Leo, the Guinea-Bissau-flagged commercial vessel involved in the tragic incident in the Odesa port, had loaded grain as cargo prior to its departure on 19 July 2026. We once again reiterate that commercial shipping and seafarers must not be targeted under any circumstances. EAM had made this position clear in the EAS and ARF meetings and reiterated with Russian FM Lavrov.
tweets.content:
On death of Indian Seafarers

Full statement:

As per information made available to us from the Directorate General of Maritime Administration, India, MV Golden Leo, the Guinea-Bissau-flagged commercial vessel involved in the tragic incident in the Odesa port, had loaded grain as cargo prior to its departure on 19 July 2026. We once again reiterate that commercial shipping and seafarers must not be targeted under any circumstances. EAM had made this position clear in the EAS and ARF meetings and reiterated with Russian FM Lavrov.

tweets.title: R to @sidhant: On Pak Claims on Flooding India statement Pakistani media reports alleging that India is deliberately causing flooding is baseless and contrary to the facts. The recent increase in flows in the River Chenab is a direct consequence of intense monsoon rainfall over Jammu and adjoining catchment areas during 20–23 July 2026. Significantly, Pakistan's own Flood Forecasting Division, Lahore, in its Flood Advisory issued on 22 July 2026 (1718 PST), attributed the high flood levels in the River Chenab to heavy rainfall over the upper catchment and stated that high flows at Marala were expected to persist before gradually receding as rainfall over the catchment decreased. The rise in river flows is, therefore, a natural hydrological response to heavy monsoon rainfall and not the result of any deliberate action by India. Attempts to portray a weather-driven flood event as an upstream intervention are factually incorrect, technically untenable, and contradicted by Pakistan's own official flood advisories. As regards allegations relating to flood warnings, the river flows during this period did not reach extraordinary discharge levels requiring the issuance of special flood warnings. The observed flows were consistent with prevailing monsoon conditions over the upper catchments, as acknowledged by Pakistan's own flood forecasting authorities. Any suggestion that India deliberately withheld flood-related information is, therefore, without factual basis. Nevertheless, like last year, India will share high-flood data with Pakistan on humanitarian grounds, through diplomatic channels as and when a situation arises.
tweets.content:
On Pak Claims on Flooding

India statement

Pakistani media reports alleging that India is deliberately causing flooding is baseless and contrary to the facts. The recent increase in flows in the River Chenab is a direct consequence of intense monsoon rainfall over Jammu and adjoining catchment areas during 20–23 July 2026.

Significantly, Pakistan's own Flood Forecasting Division, Lahore, in its Flood Advisory issued on 22 July 2026 (1718 PST), attributed the high flood levels in the River Chenab to heavy rainfall over the upper catchment and stated that high flows at Marala were expected to persist before gradually receding as rainfall over the catchment decreased.

The rise in river flows is, therefore, a natural hydrological response to heavy monsoon rainfall and not the result of any deliberate action by India. Attempts to portray a weather-driven flood event as an upstream intervention are factually incorrect, technically untenable, and contradicted by Pakistan's own official flood advisories.

As regards allegations relating to flood warnings, the river flows during this period did not reach extraordinary discharge levels requiring the issuance of special flood warnings. The observed flows were consistent with prevailing monsoon conditions over the upper catchments, as acknowledged by Pakistan's own flood forecasting authorities. Any suggestion that India deliberately withheld flood-related information is, therefore, without factual basis.
Nevertheless, like last year, India will share high-flood data with Pakistan on humanitarian grounds, through diplomatic channels as and when a situation arises.
tweets.author: @sidhant

tweets.title: Following the confusion created after Vice Admiral Puri’s remarks, we’ve decided to do the difficult but necessary work through the Astra Kosh Initiative. The goal is to build a comprehensive, collaborative encyclopedia of Indian weapon systems. An organized reference where contributors can add, verify, and refine information over time. The idea is simple: make facts clear, well-documented, and easily accessible so discussions are based on evidence rather than fragmented or conflicting information. Over the coming weeks, I’ll be reaching out to many of you for technical inputs, historical details, and source material. Together, we can build a reliable reference for the entire defence community. Further request to veterans in this field (tagged - Though I don’t like tagging): if a few of you could help guide and mentor this effort (information part - just the guidance what we should refer), it would make the project far stronger and benefit the wider defence community. @sanjeev__kapoor @GauravT71548031 @AnchitGupta9 @shiv_cybersurg @Indrani1_Roy @Firezstarter1 @KesariDhwaj @ReviewVayu @hellfire_81 And then entire DefTwitter : We cannot do anything without your support so will you please help ? As of now we have skeleton in place and information is based on popular belief (without vetting).
tweets.content:
Following the confusion created after Vice Admiral Puri’s remarks, we’ve decided to do the difficult but necessary work through the Astra Kosh Initiative.

The goal is to build a comprehensive, collaborative encyclopedia of Indian weapon systems. An organized reference where contributors can add, verify, and refine information over time.

The idea is simple: make facts clear, well-documented, and easily accessible so discussions are based on evidence rather than fragmented or conflicting information.

Over the coming weeks, I’ll be reaching out to many of you for technical inputs, historical details, and source material. Together, we can build a reliable reference for the entire defence community.

Further request to veterans in this field (tagged - Though I don’t like tagging): if a few of you could help guide and mentor this effort (information part - just the guidance what we should refer), it would make the project far stronger and benefit the wider defence community.

@sanjeev__kapoor @GauravT71548031 @AnchitGupta9 @shiv_cybersurg @Indrani1_Roy @Firezstarter1 @KesariDhwaj @ReviewVayu @hellfire_81

And then entire DefTwitter : We cannot do anything without your support so will you please help ?

As of now we have skeleton in place and information is based on popular belief (without vetting).
tweets.author: @alpha_defense

tweets.title: R to @sidhant: Transcript: EAM said that when it comes to sovereignty issues, it is actually India that should be and is raising them. India does not violate China’s sovereignty in any manner and has been consistent. But China is in illegal occupation of Indian territory in Jammu & Kashmir and Ladakh since 1963, where India’s right is officially acknowledged even by China. Also, there are Chinese projects on territories in Jammu and Kashmir that belong to India, and we object to that.
tweets.content:
Transcript:

EAM said that when it comes to sovereignty issues, it is actually India that should be and is raising them. India does not violate China’s sovereignty in any manner and has been consistent. But China is in illegal occupation of Indian territory in Jammu & Kashmir and Ladakh since 1963, where India’s right is officially acknowledged even by China. Also, there are Chinese projects on territories in Jammu and Kashmir that belong to India, and we object to that.

tweets.title: R to @NetramDefence: 14/ A Lesson in Geopolitics The Siliguri Corridor illustrates one of geopolitics’ oldest realities: geography shapes strategy. A strip of land barely 20 km wide influences military planning, infrastructure development, diplomacy, disaster management, economic resilience and national security for an entire region
tweets.content:
14/

A Lesson in Geopolitics

The Siliguri Corridor illustrates one of geopolitics’ oldest realities: geography shapes strategy. A strip of land barely 20 km wide influences military planning, infrastructure development, diplomacy, disaster management, economic resilience and national security for an entire region
tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title: R to @NetramDefence: 15/ Conclusion The Siliguri Corridor is far more than a narrow passage on the map. It is India’s strategic lifeline to the Northeast, a corridor where geography, history, diplomacy and military preparedness converge. Protecting this vital artery through robust defence, resilient infrastructure and diversified connectivity remains one of India’s foremost strategic imperatives in the 21st century.
tweets.content:
15/

Conclusion

The Siliguri Corridor is far more than a narrow passage on the map. It is India’s strategic lifeline to the Northeast, a corridor where geography, history, diplomacy and military preparedness converge. Protecting this vital artery through robust defence, resilient infrastructure and diversified connectivity remains one of India’s foremost strategic imperatives in the 21st century.
tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title: THREAD🧵 Significance of Siliguri Corridor Few geographical features carry as much strategic importance as the Siliguri Corridor, popularly known as the “Chicken’s Neck.” This narrow strip of land in northern West Bengal is the only continuous land bridge connecting mainland India to its eight northeastern states. Every military planner, policymaker and strategist recognizes it as one of India’s most critical national security vulnerabilities. @adgpi @AN_Command @NorthernComd_IA @IAF_MCC @westerncomd_I @indiannavy @easterncomd @official_dg @IaSouthern @HQ_IDS_India @IN_HQENC @SpokespersonMoD @IN_WNC @DefenceMinIndia @rajnathsingh @rashtrapatibhvn 1/
tweets.content:
THREAD🧵

Significance of Siliguri Corridor

Few geographical features carry as much strategic importance as the Siliguri Corridor, popularly known as the “Chicken’s Neck.” This narrow strip of land in northern West Bengal is the only continuous land bridge connecting mainland India to its eight northeastern states. Every military planner, policymaker and strategist recognizes it as one of India’s most critical national security vulnerabilities.

tweets.title: Hear the man himself: Satyanarayan Nuwal, Chairman, Solar Defence & Aerospace Ltd. (SDAL): “Bhargavastra is a complete system. Internal trials will finish in 2–3 months. The micro-missile has already been tested at Balasore and Pokhran, and the integrated system, including radar, EOTS, and missile has been validated. Our radar has a 10 km range and the EOTS 6 km, exceeding the Army’s current 2.5 km requirement. We have already begun work on a 6 km interceptor and are confident of realizing the PM’s ‘Sudarshan Chakra’ vision by 2030 with a layered 2.5 km, 6 km, and 25 km air defence system.”
tweets.content:
Hear the man himself: Satyanarayan Nuwal, Chairman, Solar Defence & Aerospace Ltd. (SDAL):

“Bhargavastra is a complete system. Internal trials will finish in 2–3 months. The micro-missile has already been tested at Balasore and Pokhran, and the integrated system, including radar, EOTS, and missile has been validated. Our radar has a 10 km range and the EOTS 6 km, exceeding the Army’s current 2.5 km requirement. We have already begun work on a 6 km interceptor and are confident of realizing the PM’s ‘Sudarshan Chakra’ vision by 2030 with a layered 2.5 km, 6 km, and 25 km air defence system.”


Video

tweets.title: RT by @OsintTV: मिलिट्री इंटेलिजेंस के इनपुट पर बीकानेर में बड़ी कार्रवाई, डीएसटी और सदर थाना पुलिस ने दो मादक पदार्थ तस्करों को दबोचा, 106 ग्राम एमडीएमए बरामद, दो लाख रुपये से अधिक की ड्रग्स जब्त, पूरे तस्करी नेटवर्क और सप्लाई चेन की जांच तेज Military Intelligence-Led Operation Busts MDMA Racket in Bikaner, Two Attested Acting on intelligence inputs from Military Intelligence, a joint team of Military Intelligence, the District Special Team (DST), and Sadar Police arrested two alleged drug traffickers in Bikaner. Around 106 grams of MDMA, valued at over ₹2 lakh, was seized during the operation. Police are now probing the wider drug supply network and possible links to other suspects. #Bikaner #MilitaryIntelligence #MDMA #DrugBust #Narcotics #DrugTrafficking #RajasthanPolice #DST #SadarPolice #CrimeNews #IndiaNews #BreakingNews #LawEnforcement #Operation #AntiDrugCampaign #Security #DrugFreeIndia #TINNetwork
tweets.content:
मिलिट्री इंटेलिजेंस के इनपुट पर बीकानेर में बड़ी कार्रवाई, डीएसटी और सदर थाना पुलिस ने दो मादक पदार्थ तस्करों को दबोचा, 106 ग्राम एमडीएमए बरामद, दो लाख रुपये से अधिक की ड्रग्स जब्त, पूरे तस्करी नेटवर्क और सप्लाई चेन की जांच तेज
Military Intelligence-Led Operation Busts MDMA Racket in Bikaner, Two Attested
Acting on intelligence inputs from Military Intelligence, a joint team of Military Intelligence, the District Special Team (DST), and Sadar Police arrested two alleged drug traffickers in Bikaner. Around 106 grams of MDMA, valued at over ₹2 lakh, was seized during the operation. Police are now probing the wider drug supply network and possible links to other suspects.

#Bikaner #MilitaryIntelligence #MDMA #DrugBust #Narcotics #DrugTrafficking #RajasthanPolice #DST #SadarPolice #CrimeNews #IndiaNews #BreakingNews #LawEnforcement #Operation #AntiDrugCampaign #Security #DrugFreeIndia #TINNetwork

Pakistan's military leadership is profiting from a lucrative, off-the-books oil smuggling ring with Iran. According to new reports, Army Chief Asim Munir used this covert joint venture as leverage to shield Saudi Arabia from Iranian missiles. The deal effectively secures massive private dividends for the generals and diplomatic points for the military.

tweets.title: Telangana HC Orders Army to Guard Disputed Land In an extraordinary order, the Telangana High Court directed the Indian Army to deploy personnel to protect a 40-acre disputed property after alleging repeated violations of court orders by HYDRAA. Justice Anil Kumar said the court had "lost faith in the state and its instrumentalities" and asked the Registrar General to coordinate with the Army for the deployment of 10 personnel. The court also warned that HYDRAA Commissioner A. Ranganath could face custody for alleged repeated contempt of court. The State government has denied the allegations, and the land ownership dispute remains pending before the court.
tweets.content:
Telangana HC Orders Army to Guard Disputed Land

In an extraordinary order, the Telangana High Court directed the Indian Army to deploy personnel to protect a 40-acre disputed property after alleging repeated violations of court orders by HYDRAA.

Justice Anil Kumar said the court had "lost faith in the state and its instrumentalities" and asked the Registrar General to coordinate with the Army for the deployment of 10 personnel.

The court also warned that HYDRAA Commissioner A. Ranganath could face custody for alleged repeated contempt of court. The State government has denied the allegations, and the land ownership dispute remains pending before the court.

tweets.title: INDIA EYES MYANMAR'S RARE EARTHS TO REDUCE DEPENDENCE ON CHINA India is deepening cooperation with Myanmar to secure access to critical rare earth minerals used in EVs, defence systems, aerospace and clean energy technologies, as New Delhi looks to diversify supply chains dominated by China. Indian delegations have visited Myanmar, while companies including IREL and other private firms are exploring opportunities in the country's rare earth sector. However, key deposits lie in Kachin State, where several mining areas are controlled by ethnic armed groups, and China still dominates the processing and refining ecosystem.
tweets.content:
INDIA EYES MYANMAR'S RARE EARTHS TO REDUCE DEPENDENCE ON CHINA

India is deepening cooperation with Myanmar to secure access to critical rare earth minerals used in EVs, defence systems, aerospace and clean energy technologies, as New Delhi looks to diversify supply chains dominated by China.

Indian delegations have visited Myanmar, while companies including IREL and other private firms are exploring opportunities in the country's rare earth sector.

However, key deposits lie in Kachin State, where several mining areas are controlled by ethnic armed groups, and China still dominates the processing and refining ecosystem.

For

Argument For

tweets.title: So admiral was confused between GE 404 and F414
tweets.content:
So admiral was confused between GE 404 and F414

chakranewz (@chakranewz)

We owe our audience a correction and an apology.

In a recent post promoting our conversation with Vice Admiral Raman Puri (Retd.), we incorrectly referred to the GE F404 engine. The reference should have been to the GE F414.

The error arose because the guest inadvertently misspoke during the conversation, and we failed to identify and correct it before publication. That is an editorial failure on our part.

We sincerely apologise for the mistake. Accuracy is fundamental to our work, and we regret that we fell short of that standard. We are reviewing our editorial processes and will endeavour to ensure that such errors do not occur again.

Thank you to those who pointed out the mistake and continue to hold us to high standards.

#KaveriEngine #IndianAirForce #Tejas #AMCA #DRDO #ChakraNewz
@SandeepUnnithan @awasthi_bob @i_DeepakBhadana

— https://nitter.net/chakranewz/status/2080297093550510335#m

tweets.title: Pakistanis often reject the “beggar” label, but this discussion tells a different story. Anchor: Pakistan has demanded a $10 billion brokerage fee from the US (for brokering a deal between the US and Iran) Guest 1: “Bohot, bohot acha kiya Pakistan ne unse maang kar.” I hope Pakistan has no intention of returning this money. This is the least the world owes us. If we ask America today, not just $10 billion, we might get a lot more. We should demand money from other countries too. Guest 2: “Americans, every bill is accounted for.” People ask, what are we getting in return for this mediation? If we are putting in so much effort, what are we getting back? A mediation role comes with a price tag now. ..😄 Video Courtesy: Hum news
tweets.content:
Pakistanis often reject the “beggar” label, but this discussion tells a different story.

Anchor: Pakistan has demanded a $10 billion brokerage fee from the US (for brokering a deal between the US and Iran)

Guest 2: “Americans, every bill is accounted for.” People ask, what are we getting in return for this mediation? If we are putting in so much effort, what are we getting back?

पेपर लीक के सभी मामलों में दोषियों को जल्द से जल्द कठोर दंड देने के लिए, केस के त्वरित निस्तारण के लिए फास्ट ट्रैक कोर्ट्स का गठन किया जाएगा। इस संबंध में निर्देश दे दिए गए हैं कि संबंधित विभाग आवश्यक कार्रवाई सुनिश्चित करें।

विद्यार्थियों के हितों को सुरक्षित करने के लिए, सरकार द्वारा लिए जा रहे निर्णयों की श्रृंखला में ये एक महत्वपूर्ण कदम है।

युवाओं के भविष्य के साथ खिलवाड़ करने वाले किसी भी व्यक्ति को बख्शा नहीं जाएगा।

tweets.title: Most police forces in the country are overworked, underequipped, and outnumbered. Delhi Police is outnumbered when it comes to tackling a protest of 1 lakh+ people, which is now growing every day. The total strength of Delhi Police is around 83,000 personnel, responsible for policing 1,484 square kilometres. In peacetime, one Delhi Police personnel is outnumbered by approximately 259 civilians, let alone during a protest of this scale. A mob tends to develop a mentality to flank and attack when it sees security forces being outnumbered. To assert control when outnumbered, they have to use force. Additional reinforcement from the CRPF now seems necessary to control the situation, considering that everything is happening in a highly sensitive zone of the country.
tweets.content:
Most police forces in the country are overworked, underequipped, and outnumbered.

Delhi Police is outnumbered when it comes to tackling a protest of 1 lakh+ people, which is now growing every day. The total strength of Delhi Police is around 83,000 personnel, responsible for policing 1,484 square kilometres.

In peacetime, one Delhi Police personnel is outnumbered by approximately 259 civilians, let alone during a protest of this scale.

A mob tends to develop a mentality to flank and attack when it sees security forces being outnumbered.

To assert control when outnumbered, they have to use force.

Additional reinforcement from the CRPF now seems necessary to control the situation, considering that everything is happening in a highly sensitive zone of the country.
